name: Kamala HQ
subject: Did you see this in Politico?
body:
Did you see the news? According to POLITICO, presidential candidate Mayor
Pete Buttigieg raised $7 million in the month of April alone.

This news comes after Joe Biden raised $6.3 million on the very first day
of his campaign.

We knew other candidates in this race would raise tremendous amounts of
money. So we’ve built our budget strategically -- right down to the last
dollar. But we do need to demonstrate our grassroots strength with the
crucial FEC deadline right around the corner.

Here’s where you come in. Right now, we have some catching up to do if
we’re going to reach our fundraising goal before the FEC deadline at the
end of June. And with the first Democratic debates of this primary
election around the corner, every single donation helps a lot. Can you
pitch in today?

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation will go through immediately:

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-ema-190617-nd?refcode=ema190617-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amount=5&express_lane=true&t=1001&refcode2=5331_5755789_aEArif&akid=5331%2E5755789%2EaEArif ]Chip in $5 now

In a few days, Kamala is hitting the road for South Carolina again, where
she’ll hear directly from voters about the issues that matter to their
communities.

We want her to stay focused on campaigning -- not worrying about
fundraising.

— Team Kamala

name: Shepard Nevel, Team Hickenlooper
subject: [SIGN NOW] We can't delay action on the climate crisis
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hickenlooper 2020
body:
| | | [](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637717d6/691c9bd/6df6f93f/11a4cfbb/165306670/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_template")
---
| Friend, I'm Shepard, I'm on the policy team here at Team Hickenlooper, and we just helped John roll out his plan to combat climate change. It's bold, unique, and I can't wait to tell you more--but first, will you stand with John to amplify his voice on this issue? **Climate change is the defining challenge of our time, and we need a bold, nationwide mobilization plan to avert the consequences to our planet. Add your name now if you agree that our next president needs to make this a priority.** [](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637717d6/691c9bd/6df6f93f/11a4cfb8/165306670/VEsE/ "http://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/s/make-the-climate-crisis-a-priority?source=em_20190617") A lot of ideas to fight climate change have been making the headlines lately, including the soundbite-friendly "Green New Deal." But John refuses to campaign on an easy fix or on promises he can't execute _on_. As he likes to say, great ideas are a dime a dozen: what matters is what you do. And with the clock running out for our planet, nothing matters more than what we can actually get done in Washington. **John 's the only candidate in this primary who has actually brought people--often people on polar opposite ends of the spectrum--together to pursue bold, concrete solutions to protect our planet. Having done it in Colorado, he's ready to do it for our nation.** Fundamental to John's approach is _collaboration_. We're not going to solve the climate crisis with massive expansions of government, or with extreme rhetoric that triggers backlash from our opponents and costs us even more valuable time to take action. Just as an example: As Governor of Colorado, by taking the opposite tack and bringing opposing parties to the table, John passed the strictest methane regulations in the country--_and_ got the oil and gas industry to pay for it. We have to work together to fight this crisis: Democrats, Republicans, government, the private sector, environmental activists, the energy industry, developing and developed nations alike. It starts with:
* **Rejoining the Paris Agreement,** re-forging our relationships with our global climate partners, and building climate goals into trade agreements and even foreign aid and military agreements
* **Treating the private sector as indispensable partners for climate action** and boldly unleashing market forces to discover the technological and infrastructural solutions we need
* **Putting a scientific approach first:** Solving this crisis will take listening to and learning from the best minds from every corner of the world. As the only trained scientist in the primary, John will make sure all climate change efforts are based on the best available data and worldwide scientific consensus.

[_**Want to get into the nitty-gritty details? Read John 's full plan here, and don't forget to add your name below.**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637717d6/691c9bd/6df6f93f/11a4cfb9/165306670/VEsF/ "https://medium.com/@johnhickenlooper/governor-hickenloopers-bold-global-plan-to-combat-climate-change-ab47df798792") [](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637717d6/691c9bd/6df6f93f/11a4cfb6/165306670/VEsC/ "https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/s/make-the-climate-crisis-a-priority?source=em_20190617") We can't wait to share more of John's policies with you as they're rolled out. Stay tuned. Thanks, Shepard Shepard Nevel
Senior Policy Advisor
Hickenlooper 2020
[](https://secure.hickenlooper. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_pet_20190617_SN_20190617-pet-SN-climatebump-all_all_contribbutton&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=&occupation=&employer=")
---
| | Paid for by Hickenlooper 2020
---

PO Box 6377, Denver, CO 80206
Contributions or gifts to Hickenlooper 2020 are not tax deductible.
If you got this message from a friend, you can join the Hickenlooper 2020 team by **[_signing up right here_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637717d6/691c9bd/6df6f93f/11a4cfb4/165306670/VEsA/ "https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/s/join-in")**.
Keep us company on the campaign trail by joining us on [_**Facebook**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637717d6/691c9bd/6df6f93f/11a4cfb5/165306670/VEsB/ "https://www.facebook.com/JohnHickenlooper/"), [_**Twitter**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637717d6/691c9bd/6df6f93f/11a4cfb2/165306670/VEsO/ "https://twitter.com/hickenlooper"), [_**Instagram**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637717d6/691c9bd/6df6f93f/11a4cfb3/165306670/VEsP/ "https://www.instagram.com/johnhickenlooper/"), and [_**YouTube**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637717d6/691c9bd/6df6f93f/11a4cfb0/165306670/VEsHBQ/ "https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC82O0Byp_ny13O0LkO1UpUg/featured").
We love staying connected to you--but if at any point you'd like to opt out, you can do so right [here](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637717d6/691c9bd/6df6f93f/11a4cfb1/165306670/VEsHBA/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com").
If we're not reaching you at the correct email address, please [_**update your information here**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637717d6/691c9bd/6df6f93f/11a4cfb4/165306670/VEsHBw/ "https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/s/join-in").

body:
Peter - It's been 112 days since the House passed an historic measure requiring universal background checks on all firearm purchases. I was proud to introduce this bill, because the scourge of gun violence on our communities has gone on for too long.

And even though 97% of Americans support background checks, Mitch McConnell and Senate Republicans still have not taken action on this pressing issue.

**Americans have made it very clear that they support universal background checks.** [_Will you add your name to our petition and demand McConnell bring background checks up for a vote in the Senate?_](https://act.myngp.com/el/3rlfo2_ocnHJp98A2cofzHW-pQ8B51lG56OdugdBHsk=/U8RdXO3UX8s6iYWAW8e_8gfGPitgbX_o59w5rG2vDTs=)

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/3rlfo2_ocnHJp98A2cofzHW-pQ8B51lG56OdugdBHsk=/U8RdXO3UX8s6iYWAW8e_8gfGPitgbX_o59w5rG2vDTs=)
I've worked hard with my colleagues on both sides of the aisle to pass this important legislation to make our communities safer from the threat of gun violence.

We can't let McConnell be the roadblock on our path to the first serious action on common sense gun violence prevention by Congress in 25 years. It's our duty to do all we can to prevent gun violence.

[_Let 's keep up the pressure on Senate Republicans to keep this legislation moving. Add your name right away!_](https://act.myngp.com/el/3rlfo2_ocnHJp98A2cofzHW-pQ8B51lG56OdugdBHsk=/U8RdXO3UX8s6iYWAW8e_8gfGPitgbX_o59w5rG2vDTs=)

Thanks for all you do,

Mike

body:
Dear Peter,

They've confronted a deep culture of corruption and abuse of power, relentlessly defended the Democratic values that you and I share, and fought tooth and nail against a surplus of bigoted and harmful legislation during the 2019 legislative session. And while a deep crisis of character continues to consume the Tennessee Republican Party, Democratic legislators are carrying the tradition of honorable and high-minded Tennessee statesmanship that every voter -- Democrat, Republican, and Independent -- expects from government.
This is why we are presenting caucus leadership with the 2019 True Grit Award at the 2019 Three Star Dinner on Saturday evening, June 22 in Nashville. We hope you will join us in honoring the dedication and resolve of the Democratic members of the Tennessee General Assembly. Tickets and sponsorships are available [here](https://act.myngp.com/el/kpy3g5D8Nzmv1QUh8TeOPR6TZPTPG-b88iCQixwNYoo=/3uumd_I3C9hSxIRZseHraKefWxdCAI4O0ciZva7CxyQ=).

Grassroots donors are the only people who make this campaign possible.

Not wealthy donors, who usually call the shots -- we don't host any exclusive events for them to whisper ideas for tax loopholes in her ear.

Not federal lobbyists or corporate PACs, who have a wish list for the next president -- we don't take a dime from any of them.

**As president, Elizabeth won't owe any favors to any powerful special interests, because she's not powered by them, she's powered by us. She'll fight to level the playing field for working people -- to give everyone a fighting chance at the American dream.

We've got a plan to bring big, structural change to Washington.

But first, there's one plan that makes all of the other plans possible: Building a strong grassroots movement in Pennsylvania and all across the country.

We're going to take power away from the big banks, giant corporations, and powerful special interests. We'll put it in the hands of the people -- there's more of us than there are of them.

name: Andrew Yang
subject: Random Man Runs for President
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Andrew Yang
body:
| | Friend --
[](http://my.yang2020.com/?e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=061819_newsletter_1&n=1) "Random Man Runs for President" was the headline of the recent Washington Post Magazine cover story about me and the campaign. The theme of the story is that I am a fairly normal guy who is now running for President and changing the conversation. I like the article a lot. The journalist did a wonderful job. The fact is, though, that I’m NOT normal. And that’s sort of the point. The average American lives in Ohio or Maine (the average states) and has a net worth of $36k, $6k if you exclude home and vehicle equity. He or she attended one year of college or post-secondary school and would struggle with an unexpected $500 bill. THAT’s normal. Kyle Christensen, who moved back to Iowa Falls to care for his ailing mother Pam and is doing contract jobs to make ends meet – he’s normal. Jodie Fassi of Goffstown, New Hampshire, who cleans homes to help pay her daughter’s college bills – she’s normal. A while back, I wrote about Richard Ojeda, a retired Major from West Virginia who declared for the Presidency in February. He ended his campaign a little while later saying, “It’s impossible for a normal guy to run for President these days.” He’s right. For a politician or presidential candidate, I seem fairly normal. But I have no illusions that I’m a regular guy or the average American. The average American couldn’t drop everything and decide to run for President. If they did, they would likely attract no attention. And the bills would catch up very quickly. That’s what happened to Richard. I’ve had a very fortunate life and am thrilled to do my part to push this country in the right direction. This campaign is about one thing – improving the life of the average American. **When someone like me is considered normal, it’s a bad sign for our democracy. Too many Americans feel like their voices are unimportant.** Let’s change that. Let’s send a message that changes the course of history. Let’s win, pass the Freedom Dividend, and improve our way of life. -Andrew

clean_body: | | Friend -- "Random Man Runs for President" was the headline of the recent Washington Post Magazine cover story about me and the campaign. The theme of the story is that I am a fairly normal guy who is now running for President and changing the conversation. I like the article a lot. The journalist did a wonderful job. The fact is, though, that I’m NOT normal. And that’s sort of the point. The average American lives in Ohio or Maine (the average states) and has a net worth of $36k, $6k if you exclude home and vehicle equity. He or she attended one year of college or post-secondary school and would struggle with an unexpected $500 bill. THAT’s normal. Kyle Christensen, who moved back to Iowa Falls to care for his ailing mother Pam and is doing contract jobs to make ends meet – he’s normal. Jodie Fassi of Goffstown, New Hampshire, who cleans homes to help pay her daughter’s college bills – she’s normal. A while back, I wrote about Richard Ojeda, a retired Major from West Virginia who declared for the Presidency in February. He ended his campaign a little while later saying, “It’s impossible for a normal guy to run for President these days.” He’s right. For a politician or presidential candidate, I seem fairly normal. But I have no illusions that I’m a regular guy or the average American. The average American couldn’t drop everything and decide to run for President. If they did, they would likely attract no attention. And the bills would catch up very quickly. That’s what happened to Richard. I’ve had a very fortunate life and am thrilled to do my part to push this country in the right direction. This campaign is about one thing – improving the life of the average American. When someone like me is considered normal, it’s a bad sign for our democracy. Too many Americans feel like their voices are unimportant. Let’s change that. Let’s send a message that changes the course of history. Let’s win, pass the Freedom Dividend, and improve our way of life. -Andrew Can you make a donation today? subject: President Trump Could Revolutionize the Private Health Insurance Market
body:
"For decades, economists and health-care experts have warned about flawed incentives and rising costs in America’s system..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d5d288392e&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **President Trump Could Revolutionize the Private Health Insurance Market**

“For decades, economists and health-care experts have warned about flawed incentives and rising costs in America’s system for employer-sponsored health insurance. That system, through which more than 150 million Americans get their coverage, has proved difficult to reform — until now,” healthcare expert Avik Roy writes in _The Washington Post_.

“Last week, the White House finalized a rule that allows employers to fund health reimbursement arrangements (HRAs) that can be used by workers to buy their own coverage on the individual market . . . Some in Washington want to take health insurance choices away from workers and replace them with the diktats of politicians. The Trump initiative moves in the opposite direction, placing health- care choices right where they have always belonged: in the hands of hard-working Americans.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3f20e7f5c6&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| Advisor to the President Ivanka Trump and Commerce Secretary Wilbur Ross are in Charlotte today for an American Workforce Policy Advisory Board meeting. “The American Workforce Policy Advisory Board was created last year by executive order ‘to encourage the private sector and educational institutions to combat the skills crisis by investing in and increasing demand-driven education,’” Brian Murphy writes in [_**The Charlotte Observer**_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f4b1cbba9d&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
| “USMCA improves virtually every component of the old NAFTA, and Florida’s agriculture industry stands to gain significantly,” Secretary of Agriculture Sonny Perdue writes in the [_Tallahassee Democrat_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=69b30ff36b&e=3ee4985eea). “Certain folks have been saying USMCA will not protect Florida farmers from cheap Mexican fruits and vegetables. They neglect to mention farmers already face such competition under NAFTA. And they overlook the ways the administration is fighting for a level playing field.”
---
|
---
| “Lockheed Martin said Monday that [they] will spend $142 million and hire 326 new workers over the next few years as it expands its southern Arkansas facility,” Andrew DeMillo reports for [_The Associated Press_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fe5a87053f&e=3ee4985eea). “The Maryland-based company announced the expansion of its plant in Camden, about 85 miles (140 kilometers) southwest of Little Rock. The facility currently employs about 700 workers.”
---
|
---
| “U.S. Commerce Secretary Wilbur Ross credited the Trump administration’s ‘America-first economic policies’ for new jobs and low unemployment rates across the country, including Virginia,” Amy Friedenberger writes in [_The Roanoke Times_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bed10b6826&e=3ee4985eea). The U.S. unemployment rate is currently 3.6 percent—the lowest in nearly 50 years—and Virginia’s has dipped even lower to 2.9 percent.
---
|

name: Teresa Tomlinson
subject: My commitment to the LGBTQ community
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TERESA TOMLINSON FOR SENATE
body:
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP07/1/74264/images/tt2020%20email%20header.png]
Dear Friend –

I’ve been standing up for LGBTQ rights since before I even knew what it meant.

I’ll explain: Tonya is more than just my sister – she’s my heart, and I knew who she was by the time she was 21⁄2 years old. At that young age, I didn’t fully understand sexuality or gender dynamics and I certainly didn’t understand why she would be bullied or mocked. But I knew that as a big sister, it was my job to stand up for her, so that’s what I did.

[https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/iPDdKvL0EAVXBH3PtHtI2FLUTvVur-hTl6Tz8qOcnDphT3MZowG78LpsOIE-JONbIVTFXAesfWJmkWTUAfJ7ZOPjbH7gzrbJf4K2TQPJCeRYydJG_su6mi42_abKXvi1ckSY5Tnj]

There’s a common tactic used by those who have power: they pit people against one another to acquire even more power. I saw this first as a teenager when I witnessed Republicans demonizing gay people as a tactic to win elections. They did it again in 2004 when 21 states put anti-marriage equality constitutional amendments on the ballot to save George W. Bush’s reelection by ginning up the Republican base. I stood against those amendments back then and I’ve never stopped standing up for the LGBTQ community.

Now, once again, we have an administration that is attacking the hard-fought and hard-won civil rights of the LGBTQ community. In just the last three months, this administration has:

Refused to allow U.S. embassies to fly the gay pride flag in honor of Pride month.
Redefined human rights to fit their interpretation of so-called “natural law” and “religious freedom.”
Promulgated a rule that allows healthcare providers to refuse medical treatment to LGBTQ patients.
Stopped the collection of data on LGBTQ children in foster care.
Banned transgender Americans from serving in the U.S. military.
Stated that they don’t care if the cancelation of the transgender bathroom policy will subject students to bullying.
Refused to reinstate a HUD policy that would prevent discrimination against LGBT members of our community in housing and in access to homeless shelters.
Promoted fiercely anti-gay judges, at all judicial levels, such as Brian Hagedorn of Wisconsin.

We’ve seen Republican leaders drum up misogyny, hate, and fear time and time again, in order to stir up and turn out their base. Now, with a miserably failing President who has no real accomplishments to speak of, they’re doing it again. But we are onto them and this time, we will not be played .

When I was mayor of Columbus, I declared June LGBTQ Pride Month for the first time in our history. I know the importance and significance of this celebration for so many Americans and Georgians. But celebrations alone will not preserve and expand the civil rights that generations have dedicated their lives to win and defend.

So here’s the next step: If we build a campaign based on these shared values, we will win Georgia’s U.S. Senate seat -- and the work has already started. Chip in $5 to our campaign today so that Georgia can finally have a Senator who will stand up for LGTBQ rights in Washington. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pride-month?refcode=em_hs_header&noskip=true]

We can, and must, do better.

Thank you,
Teresa

I’ll explain: Tonya is more than just my sister – she’s my heart, and I knew who she was by the time she was 21⁄2 years old. At that young age, I didn’t fully understand sexuality or gender dynamics and I certainly didn’t understand why she would be bullied or mocked. But I knew that as a big sister, it was my job to stand up for her, so that’s what I did. [] There’s a common tactic used by those who have power: they pit people against one another to acquire even more power. I saw this first as a teenager when I witnessed Republicans demonizing gay people as a tactic to win elections. They did it again in 2004 when 21 states put anti-marriage equality constitutional amendments on the ballot to save George W. Bush’s reelection by ginning up the Republican base. I stood against those amendments back then and I’ve never stopped standing up for the LGBTQ community. Now, once again, we have an administration that is attacking the hard-fought and hard-won civil rights of the LGBTQ community. In just the last three months, this administration has: Refused to allow U.S. embassies to fly the gay pride flag in honor of Pride month. Redefined human rights to fit their interpretation of so-called “natural law” and “religious freedom.” Promulgated a rule that allows healthcare providers to refuse medical treatment to LGBTQ patients. Stopped the collection of data on LGBTQ children in foster care. Banned transgender Americans from serving in the U.S. military. Stated that they don’t care if the cancelation of the transgender bathroom policy will subject students to bullying. Refused to reinstate a HUD policy that would prevent discrimination against LGBT members of our community in housing and in access to homeless shelters. Promoted fiercely anti-gay judges, at all judicial levels, such as Brian Hagedorn of Wisconsin. We’ve seen Republican leaders drum up misogyny, hate, and fear time and time again, in order to stir up and turn out their base. Now, with a miserably failing President who has no real accomplishments to speak of, they’re doing it again. But we are onto them and this time, we will not be played . When I was mayor of Columbus, I declared June LGBTQ Pride Month for the first time in our history. I know the importance and significance of this celebration for so many Americans and Georgians. But celebrations alone will not preserve and expand the civil rights that generations have dedicated their lives to win and defend. So here’s the next step: If we build a campaign based on these shared values, we will win Georgia’s U.S. Senate seat -- and the work has already started. Chip in $5 to our campaign today so that Georgia can finally have a Senator who will stand up for LGTBQ rights in Washington. [] We can, and must, do better. Thank you, Teresa <<View this email in your browser.>> [] PAID FOR BY TERESA TOMLINSON FOR SENATE PMB 263, 3201 Macon Rd Ste 139, Columbus, GA 31906This message was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. President Trump is threatening to deport “millions” of undocumented immigrants starting next week. Instead of working to create real policies, the president settles for stirring the pot of racism and economic resentment. He threatens the freedom, safety, and lives of immigrants and refugees who have committed no crime other than fleeing poverty, violence, and climate change. These tactics have no place in a democratic society. They’re ripped from the favorite playbook of petty tyrants from across the world. We must not allow the Trump administration and its supporters in Congress to continue to corrupt the values and the ideals of the United States. **[Help send me to Congress to stand up for our immigrant communities.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/7619313/72253031/1269368949?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzMjViYzhiZC0xMDkyLWU5MTEtYWJjNC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KXOT5tFRbl-WXzgz_A_OAanBPV-W4k6hFBIXOqvLCAA=&refcode=em061819) ** As a city attorney, I fought Governor Greg Abbott and the Republican state legislature’s efforts to force Austin to implement inhumane immigration policies. As a member of Congress, I will fight just as hard at the federal level. Unlike the incumbent, I have never and will never take money from the for-profit prison industry, which has made millions off of caging immigrants and refugees. My stance is clear: For the people, not for profit. Scare tactics don’t create solutions. Let’s fight back by embracing our diversity and our ingenuity. Let’s work together to enact bold new policies that create jobs. Let’s build hospitals and improve our public education system. Let’s send Trump and all of his enablers in Congress a clear message: Their racist fearmongering will not be tolerated. The time for change is now. [**Can we count on you to bring change to Congress? clean_body: = Peter, This is a declaration of war. President Trump is threatening to deport “millions” of undocumented immigrants starting next week. Instead of working to create real policies, the president settles for stirring the pot of racism and economic resentment. He threatens the freedom, safety, and lives of immigrants and refugees who have committed no crime other than fleeing poverty, violence, and climate change. These tactics have no place in a democratic society. They’re ripped from the favorite playbook of petty tyrants from across the world. We must not allow the Trump administration and its supporters in Congress to continue to corrupt the values and the ideals of the United States. As a city attorney, I fought Governor Greg Abbott and the Republican state legislature’s efforts to force Austin to implement inhumane immigration policies. As a member of Congress, I will fight just as hard at the federal level. Unlike the incumbent, I have never and will never take money from the for-profit prison industry, which has made millions off of caging immigrants and refugees. My stance is clear: For the people, not for profit. Scare tactics don’t create solutions. Let’s fight back by embracing our diversity and our ingenuity. Let’s work together to enact bold new policies that create jobs. Let’s build hospitals and improve our public education system. Let’s send Trump and all of his enablers in Congress a clear message: Their racist fearmongering will not be tolerated. The time for change is now. | For the people, Mike Siegel = = =** = PO Box 2409 Austin, TX 78701

subject: It's crunch time!

body:
The time is now to step up for real conservative leadership! | | |
---
| **Add your name to the list of conservative fighters!**
---
| | This month has been a whirlwind and we are thrilled with the enthusiastic response we have received from our wonderful supporters.

We’ve got less than two weeks left in this quarter, and now it’s crunch time. Between now and **June 30 th**, we need to add as many names to our list of supporters as we can so we can once again show that we are the candidate conservative Kansans want as their next U.S. Senator.

I have met so many Kansans in the past six months that are fed up with the watered-down approach to protecting our conservative values. **_Too many of our elected leaders back down when the heat is on, but that is not how I operate._**

There is no room for compromise on important issues like [protecting life, keeping our nation’s borders secure, and ](https://laturnerforsenate.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=66e1e33aabb963218c502c41e&id=f6fcceec39&e=6882b657f8)[getting our country out of debt to secure the future for our grandchildren and great grandchildren.](https://laturnerforsenate.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=66e1e33aabb963218c502c41e&id=84dd7689dc&e=6882b657f8)

[Please help us reach our goal by the end of this month with a donation to our campaign.](https://laturnerforsenate.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=66e1e33aabb963218c502c41e&id=f50a310e49&e=6882b657f8) Last quarter we had an incredible show of support from everyday Kansans, and we need to do it again.

We have a path to victory as long as you get behind us early, sending a clear message that Kansans want nothing less than a conservative fighter in the U.S. Senate. [Would you consider contributing $10, $50, or even $100 to our campaign?](https://laturnerforsenate.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=66e1e33aabb963218c502c41e&id=4c7b469496&e=6882b657f8)

Thank you to all of you who are in your communities standing up for conservative values and fighting for the kind of Kansas we want for our children and grandchildren.

Together we can take this fight all the way to Washington D.C.!

Jake LaTurner

subject: [ PETITION ] We did our job. Help me call on the Senate to step up
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger For Congress
body:
It's been more than 100 days since I voted with my House colleagues to pass the For the People Act -- a comprehensive democracy reform package to enact strong campaign finance and good governance reforms.
| | Peter,

It's been more than 100 days since I voted with my House colleagues to pass the For the People Act -- a comprehensive democracy reform package to enact strong campaign finance and good governance reforms. We voted to affirm that we are working for them and are taking action to end the influence of dark money in politics.

**There have been plenty of opportunities for Majority Leader Mitch McConnell to bring this bill to the Senate floor for a vote, but he has not.**

Instead, he has settled for 100 days of sitting on his hands -- the kind of inaction that erodes Americans' faith in Congress. Every day that this critical legislation goes untouched in the Senate, we get further and further from fulfilling the will of the people. **Peter -- We did our job by passing this critical legislation. It 's time for the Senate to start working for the people and do theirs.

[**I need your help to get this legislation on the floor of the Senate for a vote: Will you add your name as a citizen co-sponsor and demand action on the For the People Act? >>**](https://act.myngp.com/el/hZNIoOIk9ui6BM_qImtOoSn8Qp0oa64Ja2TWc0x8fT8=/EesAtETQXXZK7gJ4fH7htg7nfqyX9ppqjuZPWYafKpw=)** ****I 'm urging Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell to take up our anti-corruption legislation and fix what's broken in Washington. **

disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for America
body:
We’re launching our official FEC deadline grassroots fundraising competition. We’ve created a personal link just for you to raise money from people in your network.
#
I’m _really_ excited for this.
You already know that Beto has an important FEC fundraising deadline at the end of this month. You know that we’ve never accepted a dime from PACs, corporations, or DC lobbyists. You know that, from the very beginning, our campaign has been funded by the grassroots.
With our biggest FEC deadline coming up fast, we need to show Beto can raise enough money to compete with the other candidates when we file our public report. So today, we’re launching our official FEC deadline grassroots fundraising competition.
We’ve created a personal link just for you to raise money from people in your network. We’ll be sending Beto the names of everyone participating. **Two people will win a chance to meet Beto:** the supporter who recruits the most contributors to donate via their link AND the supporter who earns the most money on their link.
Here’s how it works:
1. **Your personal link tracks all donations that come through it.** Copy that link now:

<https://betoorourke.com/grassroots/dpwillis67@gmail.com>
---
2. **Send your personal donation link via email or text message to your friends and family.** Ask them to pitch in through your crowdfunding page. If it helps, you can tell them the average contribution online to Beto’s campaign is about $25.

Later this week, we’ll report back on how much money you’ve raised via your personal link (and how many people donated). We have a critical deadline coming up on June 30, so that’s the last day to get someone to contribute through your link and win the contest.
**Get started now by copying your link.** You can donate yourself, or get a friend to donate, to kick it off:
<https://betoorourke.com/grassroots/dpwillis67@gmail.com>
---
I can’t wait to see how this works.
Jen O’Malley Dillon
Campaign Manager
Beto for America

Paid for by Beto for America

To contribute via check, please address to Beto for America campaign, P.O. Box 3628 El Paso, TX 79923.

Tomorrow marks an important deadline for my campaign. | | | | [ ](https://drjoanperry.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=40b941504fba213abdaddc0bc&id=2221eaf03e&e=0bf1d0f47f)

| Friends -

Tomorrow marks a critical deadline for my campaign -- the end of a fundraising quarter. We will have to file a report showing how much we have raised. And it needs to be strong. [Can you help](https://drjoanperry.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=40b941504fba213abdaddc0bc&id=d9e498ff28&e=0bf1d0f47f)?

I've been humbled by the support I've received this far, and I am very grateful for each of you.

But in order to win in three weeks on July 9th, I need to continue to raise money to get out my message to Republican voters. Since Day 1 of this Primary Runoff, I've been unfairly attacked. This finance report needs to show I have the means necessary to continue to answer these attacks. That is why I hope I can count on your support before tomorrow at midnight.

We've heard a lot of praise for our [most recent ad](https://drjoanperry.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=40b941504fba213abdaddc0bc&id=d6972df316&e=0bf1d0f47f). Our message is resonating with voters in the district. Your continued support means we can reach more voters in these final weeks while they’re deciding.

You can donate [online](https://drjoanperry.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=40b941504fba213abdaddc0bc&id=95101d50a4&e=0bf1d0f47f) or mail a check to the address below.

Thank you for your continued support, prayers and friendship.

Yours truly,

Joan Perry

PS: You can help by sending this email to a few friends. Will you share my [ad](https://drjoanperry.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=40b941504fba213abdaddc0bc&id=b539d2d1e4&e=0bf1d0f47f) and why you’re supporting me? Your help could be the difference in a close race with such low turnout. Thank you!

name: Team Fair Fight
subject: From Randolph County to Lumpkin County
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fair Fight Action
body:
|
---
[](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa6ce/34a064cc/6eec4d1/703efd5/116552572/VEsH/ "https://www.fairfightgeorgia.com/")

| | | Friend - Last year, the Randolph County Board of Elections made national news when it considered a proposal to close seven of its nine polling locations ahead of the midterm elections. In areas with no public transportation, some residents would have been forced to walk over three hours to exercise their constitutional right to vote. And earlier this spring, the Randolph County Board of Commissioners revived conversations on plans that could lead to needless poll closures in 2020. Let's be clear: a proposal to eliminate polling places in a Deep South county belongs in a history textbook, not the local news. As our federal lawsuit makes clear, polling location closures haven't happened by accident in Georgia. Since the Supreme Court's ruling in _Shelby County v. Holder_ , election boards have had carte blanche to eliminate polling locations, change election rules, or propose unfair maps - just as they did before the Voting Rights Act of 1965. In Georgia, then-Secretary Brian Kemp went so far as to issue a memorandum to local elections boards in 2015 outlining why polling place closures might be appropriate, emphasizing that _Shelby County v. Holder_ removed the Voting Rights Act requirement that elections officials submit precinct or polling place changes to the U.S. Department of Justice for preclearance. So it's no surprise that since 2012, Georgia county election officials have closed 214 polling places across the state. As a result, a third of Georgia's counties now have fewer polling places than they had in 2012, and more than half of the counties impacted by these closures have been counties where poverty rates are higher than the state average or whose population is over 25 percent Black. [**If you share our belief that every voter should be able to cast their ballot without undue hardship, will you chip in to Fair Fight today and help us fight back against polling place closures?**](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa6ce/34a064cc/6eec4d1/703efd2/116552572/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/fairfightga2018-2?refcode=IE061819CLOSE") From Lumpkin County to Lanier County, from Warren County to Randolph County, we're committed to making sure every voter can access the ballot. Thank you for staying in the fight with us. Onward,
Team Fair Fight

---
| The White House • June 18, 2019
---
| | |
# **Ivanka Trump visits the factory floor in Charlotte**

**Advisor to the President Ivanka Trump** and Secretary of Commerce Wilbur Ross hosted the second meeting of the American Workforce Policy Advisory Board today in Charlotte, North Carolina. While visiting a Siemens USA manufacturing plant, Ms. Trump paid a visit to the factory floor.

**🎬****Watch:** [Ivanka Trump talks with a member of Siemens’ apprenticeship program](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b9aa123875&e=3ee4985eea)

**“When I told my parents** I was looking into this, at first they were kind of skeptical because they think of manufacturing as dirty, dingy, really dark,” said Chad Robinson, a participant in Siemens USA’s apprenticeship program. “And then they came here.”

**The reality** is that modern manufacturing is a source of good-paying, high-quality work for millions of Americans. Today, there are more than 500,000 open jobs in the manufacturing sector, according to National Association of Manufacturers President Jay Timmons. In 10 years, that figure could reach 2.4 _million_ openings. Our great workers need the skills and training to match these opportunities.

**Rather than give up** on the idea of America as a global manufacturing powerhouse, President Donald J. Trump doubled down on making that vision a reality. Last summer, he launched the Pledge to America’s Workers, which challenges companies to expand programs to educate, train, and reskill workers from high-school age up to near-retirement.

**Ivanka Trump** has led the charge, crisscrossing the United States and securing commitments from small, medium-sized, and multinational companies from coast to coast. The total number of new opportunities created as part of the Pledge to America’s Workers is [more than 9 million.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c661302198&e=3ee4985eea)

**“The U.S. is the best place** in the world for people to locate,” Secretary Ross said today about the growing American manufacturing sector. “The job now is more design, more quality control, more sophisticated work than it had been. So it’s not that there’s less work; it’s just a different kind of work and work that requires a bit more training.”

**The return of manufacturing** reflects a broader turnaround in the U.S. jobs climate under President Trump. As of May, America’s 3.6 percent unemployment rate matches its lowest level since 1969—and is the 15th straight month with unemployment at or below 4 percent. The economy has added more than 5.8 million jobs since President Trump’s election.

[Photos: Ivanka Trump’s “energizing” visit with factory workers in Charlotte](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ba3e41da18&e=3ee4985eea)

**Siemens CEO:** [We’re proud to showcase our “world-class apprenticeship program”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f4e54bec3e&e=3ee4985eea)
